body_txt: X. There'x alwayx xomething about the letter X that juxt makex x-actly anything cool (including thix artixle). A lot of people use it gratuitouxly on a name, product, model, etx. juxt xo it has xome xort of edge to it, including xere. Want xome pizzazz? Add an X somewhere in txe name. It doexn't matter where, as long as there'x an X. And it hax to be a capital X. Lowercase x is juxt too puny to be xool. Juxt don't add three of them. In order for this trope to work, though: Linguists have noted that X has a variety of different uses in English, such as x as an unknown in maths, or an x horizontal axis on a graph. X is also preferred in many countries when filling in a box in an election, or as a kiss when texting. It also shows that we play with this trope all the time : X denoting a larger clothing size (played straight); an X marking failure in an exam (inversion); or X-rated, to suggest that it's not something you would let your kids watch (which zig-zags it, as it could be Forbidden Fruit ). It can be x-specially x-tra confusing when you don't know whether X is a substitute for a checkmark or the opposite of one. Subtrope of Xtreme Kool Letterz . For tropes that make use of this and when a work sequel adds the X after the original title, see Lettered Sequel . See also 4X , Let X Be the Unknown . Other tropes with the letter X include "X" Marks the Spot , "X" Marks the Hero , and X Slash . Not to be confused with [A Metasyntactic Variable as Portrayed in This Case by X] Makes Anything Cool .
